summary: Stellar Bird Song, fire‑filled Hey Pocky Way and tight second set
blurb: The July 3 1988 Oxford Plains show delivers soaring Bird Song, a smoking Hey Pocky Way and a powerful second‑set flow that peaks with Dear Mr. Fantasy → Hey Jude finale. Jerry’s vocals are strong, Phil’s bass thunders, and Brent commands the keys. The crystal‑clear SBD makes this one of the most listenable shows of ’88.
review: On July 3, 1988 at Oxford Plains Speedway the Dead unleashed a punchy first set that built momentum into a second‑set powerhouse. Highlights include an exuberant Bird Song where Garcia smiles at the glider, a smoking Hey Pocky Way that showcases Brent’s big Hammond chops, and tight jams on Touch of Grey, Eyes Of The World and I Will Take You Home. The rhythm section stays muscular throughout the pre‑drums/space segment, leading into a rousing Drums and an emotional Dear Mr. Fantasy → Hey Jude finale that caps the night with pure joy. The crystal‑clear soundboard capture (gd88-07-03.sbd.ststephen.3908.sbeok.shnf) preserves every nuance—from Phil’s thundering bass to Mickey and Bill’s tight, energetic drumming—making this show a must‑listen for any Deadhead looking for an ’80s high point.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Six of the seven detailed recordings praise the performance with language like 'crystal‑clear', 'tight playing', 'big Brent night' and highlight multiple songs across sets. No consistent negative comments about musicianship appear; only a few isolated poor‑song mentions. This strong, uniform positivity yields a robust +1.6 sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear soundboard with excellent mix, strong bass extension and minimal hiss; captures the band’s tight playing and all standout songs.

key_highlights: Crystal‑clear SBD captures soaring Bird Song and smoking Hey Pocky Way.
Tight Rhythm Devils give a muscular pre‑drums/space segment and energetic drum circle.
Brent Mydland delivers a ‘big Brent night’ with standout Hammond work and piano solo in Eyes Of The World.
Powerful second‑set flow ends with an emotional Dear Mr. Fantasy → Hey Jude finale.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s vocals are strong and excited, especially on Dear Mr. Fantasy; his guitar tone is good and he’s visibly enjoying the show.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh provides thundering bass with excellent extension and clean presence, despite a heavy mix for some listeners.
band_performance.Bob: Weir’s pink Telecaster/Modulus Pepto adds energetic rhythm work; occasional roughness noted but overall solid.
band_performance.Brent: Mydland dominates the night – big Hammond on Walking Blues, standout piano solo in Eyes Of The World, and driving performance on Hey Pocky Way earn high praise.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart delivers a tight pre‑drums/space segment and contributes muscular drumming throughout the set.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann’s energetic drumming complements Mickey, especially during the drum circle and second‑set climaxes.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
